revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 4 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 4-ngie̍t]][[ja:4月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 4월]][[th:เดือน 4]][[wuu:农历四月]][[za:Ndwenseiq]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 4 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:四月]][[zh:农历四月]]
{{Lunisolar April|qq=}} '''Lunisolar April''' (旧暦四月) is the fourth {{ill|Month|en|Month|qid=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|qid=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}). Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|qid=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, April was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Xiaoman|en|Xiaoman|qid=Q1190998|lt=Xiaoman|1=Xiaoman}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|April|en|April|qid=Q118|lt=April|1=April}} to early {{ill|June|en|June|qid=Q120|lt=June|1=June}}. Another name for April is {{nihongo|'''Uzuki'''|うづき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|April|ja|4月|qq=draft}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Mōka'''|もうか|qq=}}”. In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month (known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10935019}} with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days) varies each year, there are years in which April ends on the 29th and April 30th does not exist. ==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, the {{ill|April (Lunisolar)|ja|4月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar April|lt_ja=旧暦4月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Uzuki''' (うづき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for April of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=The new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125314718}}. The standard theory holds that Uzuki is an abbreviation of “Unohana-zuki” (卯の花月; うのはなづき), “month of Deutzia flowers.” However, another theory posits that because the flowers blooming in Uzuki are called “U no hana” (卯の花), the month itself was named after those flowers. Other theories beyond “Unohana-zuki” include that the fourth sign of the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|qid=Q1072961|lt=Chinese zodiac|1=Earthly Branches}} is the {{ill|Rabbit|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16662792}}, hence “Uzuki”; or that it is the month for planting rice seedlings ({{ill|Oryza sativa|en|Oryza sativa|qid=Q161426|lt=稲|1=Oryza sativa}}), thus called “Tanezuki” (種月; うづき), “Uetsuki” (植月; うゑつき), “Taunahe-zuki” (田植苗月; たうなへづき), or “Naheue-zuki” (苗植月; なへうゑづき). Another alternate name is “Natsuhazuki” (夏初月; なつはづき).
In Japan, this month is famous as the time of the new {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|qid=Q336568|lt=fiscal year|1=Year-ending}} or new {{ill|school term|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school term|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10291405}}, and in schools, {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=Town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q25550691}}, companies, etc., {{ill|entrance ceremony|ja|入学式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/entrance ceremony|1=school entrance ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11388885}} and {{ill|initiation ceremony|ja|入社式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/initiation ceremony|1=initiation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11389005}} are held, making it as hectic as March. Although the number of households and population is small, there is also the surname “'''Uzuki'''.” April always begins on the same day of the week as July, and in a {{ill|Leap year|en|Leap year|qid=Q19828|lt=leap year|1=Leap year}} also the same as January.
Since it can be easily misheard as July (しちがつ) over the phone, it is standard practice for the respondent to ask “『よんがつ』ですか?”
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, April is called “Upasrurechup” (ウパㇱルレチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: upas rure cup), meaning “month that melts snow”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|April}} comes from the Latin {{lang|la|Aprilis}}, a month dedicated to {{ill|Venus (mythology)|en|Venus (mythology)|qid=Q47652|lt=Venus|1=Venus (mythology)}} (from the Etruscan name {{lang|la|Apru}} of the corresponding Greek goddess {{ill|Aphrodite|en|Aphrodite|qid=Q35500|lt=Aphrodite|1=Aphrodite}}).
== Uzuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Uzuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|qid=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}} || {{ill|May 28|en|May 28|qid=Q2588|lt=May 28|1=May 28}} || 30 days |- | 2015 || {{ill|May 18|en|May 18|qid=Q2576|lt=May 18|1=May 18}} || {{ill|June 15|en|June 15|qid=Q2613|lt=June 15|1=June 15}} || 29 days |- | 2016 || {{ill|May 7|en|May 7|qid=Q2553|lt=May 7|1=May 7}} || {{ill|June 4|en|June 4|qid=Q2627|lt=June 4|1=June 4}} || 29 days |- | 2017 || {{ill|April 26|en|April 26|qid=Q2532|lt=April 26|1=April 26}} || {{ill|May 25|en|May 25|qid=Q2585|lt=May 25|1=May 25}} || 30 days |- | 2018 || {{ill|May 15|en|May 15|qid=Q2562|lt=May 15|1=May 15}} || {{ill|June 13|en|June 13|qid=Q2614|lt=June 13|1=June 13}} || 30 days |- | 2019 || {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|qid=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}} || {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|qid=Q2624|lt=June 2|1=June 2}} || 29 days |- | 2020 || {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|qid=Q2527|lt=April 23|1=April 23}} || {{ill|May 22|en|May 22|qid=Q2581|lt=May 22|1=May 22}} || 30 days |- | 2020 ([[:en:Leap month|leap]]) || {{ill|May 23|en|May 23|qid=Q2583|lt=May 23|1=May 23}} || {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|qid=Q2666|lt=June 20|1=June 20}} || 29 days |- | 2021 || {{ill|May 12|en|May 12|qid=Q2559|lt=May 12|1=May 12}} || {{ill|June 9|en|June 9|qid=Q2618|lt=June 9|1=June 9}} || 29 days |- | 2022 || {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|qid=Q2544|lt=May 1|1=May 1}} || {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|qid=Q2589|lt=May 29|1=May 29}} || 29 days |- | 2023 || {{ill|May 20|en|May 20|qid=Q2579|lt=May 20|1=May 20}} || {{ill|June 17|en|June 17|qid=Q2672|lt=June 17|1=June 17}} || 29 days |- | 2024 || {{ill|May 8|en|May 8|qid=Q2555|lt=May 8|1=May 8}} || {{ill|June 5|en|June 5|qid=Q2622|lt=June 5|1=June 5}} || 29 days |- | 2025 || {{ill|April 28|en|April 28|qid=Q2534|lt=April 28|1=April 28}} || {{ill|May 26|en|May 26|qid=Q2586|lt=May 26|1=May 26}} || 29 days |}
== Related Articles == {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar April|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|4|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|April 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|4月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
